verdict_reasoning: C3 is a secreted plasma protein with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or outer-leaflet lipidation. It is synthesized mainly by the liver, secreted into plasma, and activated proteolytically to C3a and C3b fragments. C3b can covalently opsonize cell surfaces via a transient thioester bond to hydroxyl/amine groups, but this deposits C3b onto target surfaces (bacteria, apoptotic cells, extracellular matrix) rather than stably anchoring the C3 protein body to the producer cell's outer leaflet — the same wash-resistance criterion that governs stable_surface_attachment. Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-surface-directed therapeutic targeting C3 on a specific cell surface; (2) no TM precursor/shedding biology; (3) thioester-mediated opsonization of non-self surfaces is matrix/stroma-like deposition, not stable PM anchoring of C3 to a TM partner; (4) no documented PM minority pool on producer cells; (5) no documented cancer/cell-death/germline/activation-induced ecto-display of the C3 protein body on the outer leaflet of producer cells. Verdict: secreted_only.
